   482→async def run_pipeline_default(
   483→    browser: BrowserManager,
   484→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   485→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   486→    location: Optional[str],
   487→    max_pages: int,
   488→    template: str,
   489→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   490→    dry_run: bool,
   491→    skip_messaged: bool,
   492→    interactive: bool = False,
   493→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   494→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
   495→) -> None:
   496→    """Pipeline: search all pages first, then send messages.
   497→
   498→    Saves a checkpoint after search completes so that message sending
   499→    can be resumed if interrupted.
   500→    """
   501→    checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   502→
   503→    if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   504→        # Keep enterprise tab open for inline messaging (unless dry_run)
   505→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   506→            browser, keywords, company, max_pages,
   507→            keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   508→        )
   509→    else:
   510→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
   511→            browser, keywords, company, location, max_pages,
   512→        )
   513→
   514→    if not all_candidates:
   515→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")
   516→        emit_progress("completed", result={"total": 0, "message": "No candidates found"})
   517→        return
   518→
   519→    # Save checkpoint after search (batch_pages=0 marks non-batch mode)
   520→    _save_checkpoint(
   521→        checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   522→        max_pages, 0, skip_messaged, interactive,
   523→        max_pages, [], seen_ids, {'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0},
   524→        mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   525→    )
   526→
   527→    display_candidates_table(all_candidates, "Pipeline Results")
   528→
   529→    # Emit search complete with candidate list for web confirmation
   530→    emit_progress("search_complete", candidates=[
   531→        {"maimai_id": c.maimai_id, "name": c.name, "title": c.title,
   532→         "company": c.company, "location": c.location or ""}
   533→        for c in all_candidates
   534→    ])
   535→
   536→    if dry_run:
   537→        console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] {len(all_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   538→        save_run_history(
   539→            keywords, template, mode,
   540→            {'total': len(all_candidates), 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0},
   541→            dry_run=True,
   542→        )
   543→        checkpoint.delete()
   544→        emit_progress("completed", result={"total": len(all_candidates), "dry_run": True})
   545→        return
   546→
   547→    if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   548→        results = await send_batch_enterprise(
   549→            all_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged,
   550→            skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   551→        )
   552→    else:
   553→        results = await send_batch(
   554→            all_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged, interactive,
   555→            skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   556→        )
   557→
   558→    # Fetch final quota for summary display
   559→    quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   560→    display_send_results(results, quota=quota)
   561→    report_path = generate_pipeline_report(all_candidates, results, keywords, template, mode)
   562→    save_run_history(keywords, template, mode, results, dry_run=False, report_path=report_path)
   563→
   564→    emit_progress("completed", result=results)
   565→
   566→    # Clean up checkpoint on successful completion
   567→    checkpoint.delete()
   568→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   569→
   570→    # Auto backup database after successful pipeline
   571→    _auto_backup_database()
   572→
   573→
   574→async def run_pipeline_batched(
   575→    browser: BrowserManager,
   576→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   577→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   578→    location: Optional[str],
   579→    max_pages: int,
   580→    batch_pages: int,
   581→    template: str,
   582→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   583→    dry_run: bool,
   584→    skip_messaged: bool,
   585→    interactive: bool = False,
   586→    resume_ckpt=None,
   587→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   588→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
   589→) -> None:
   590→    """Batched pipeline: search N pages, send messages, repeat."""
   591→    # Restore state from checkpoint or start fresh
   592→    if resume_ckpt:
   593→        # On resume, only treat sent_ids as truly "seen" (skip-worthy).
   594→        # Candidates that were found but not sent should be retried.
   595→        sent_ids = list(resume_ckpt.sent_ids)
   596→        seen_ids = set(sent_ids)  # only skip candidates already successfully sent
   597→        total_results = resume_ckpt.total_results or {
   598→            'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0,
   599→        }
   600→        start_from_page = resume_ckpt.completed_pages + 1
   601→        console.print(f"[cyan]Resuming from page {start_from_page} (sent: {len(sent_ids)}, retrying unsent)[/cyan]")
   602→        checkpoint = resume_ckpt
   603→    else:
   604→        seen_ids = set()
   605→        sent_ids = []
   606→        total_results = {'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0}
   607→        start_from_page = 1
   608→        checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   609→
   610→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   611→    all_details: List[dict] = []
   612→    batch_num = 0
   613→    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   614→
   615→    batch_start = start_from_page
   616→    while batch_start <= max_pages:
   617→        batch_page_count = min(batch_pages, max_pages - batch_start + 1)
   618→        batch_num += 1
   619→        batch_end = batch_start + batch_page_count - 1
   620→
   621→        console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Batch {batch_num}: Pages {batch_start}-{batch_end} ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   622→
   623→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   624→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   625→                browser, keywords, company, batch_page_count,
   626→                keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   627→            )
   628→        else:
   629→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords(
   630→                browser, keywords, company, location, batch_page_count, start_page=batch_start,
   631→            )
   632→        # Filter out globally seen IDs
   633→        batch_candidates = [c for c in batch_candidates if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids]
   634→        seen_ids.update(c.maimai_id for c in batch_candidates)
   635→        all_candidates.extend(batch_candidates)
   636→
   637→        if not batch_candidates:
   638→            consecutive_empty_batches += 1
   639→            console.print(f"[yellow]No new candidates in batch {batch_num}.[/yellow]")
   640→
   641→            if consecutive_empty_batches >= 2:
   642→                console.print("[red]Multiple consecutive empty batches detected. Verifying login status...[/red]")
   643→                if not await check_login_status(browser.page):
   644→                    console.print("[red]Session expired! Clearing session and re-authenticating...[/red]")
   645→                    session_manager = get_session_manager()
   646→                    await session_manager.clear()
   647→                    await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   648→                    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   649→                    console.print("[green]Re-authenticated. Retrying current batch...[/green]")
   650→                    batch_num -= 1
   651→                    continue
   652→                else:
   653→                    console.print("[yellow]Session appears valid. Possibly no more results for these keywords.[/yellow]")
   654→
   655→            _save_checkpoint(
   656→                checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   657→                max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   658→                batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   659→                mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   660→            )
   661→            batch_start += batch_pages
   662→            continue
   663→        else:
   664→            consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   665→
   666→        display_candidates_table(batch_candidates, f"Batch {batch_num} Results")
   667→
   668→        if dry_run:
   669→            console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] Batch {batch_num}: {len(batch_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   670→            _save_checkpoint(
   671→                checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   672→                max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   673→                batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   674→                mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   675→            )
   676→            batch_start += batch_pages
   677→            continue
   678→
   679→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   680→            results = await send_batch_enterprise(
   681→                batch_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged,
   682→                skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   683→            )
   684→        else:
   685→            results = await send_batch(
   686→                batch_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged, interactive,
   687→                skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   688→            )
   689→        batch_quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   690→        display_send_results(results, title=f"Batch {batch_num} Send Results", quota=batch_quota)
   691→
   692→        all_details.extend(results.get('details', []))
   693→        for detail in results.get('details', []):
   694→            if detail.get('status') == 'sent':
   695→                sent_ids.append(detail['maimai_id'])
   696→
   697→        for key in total_results:
   698→            total_results[key] += results[key]
   699→
   700→        _save_checkpoint(
   701→            checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   702→            max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   703→            batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   704→            mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   705→        )
   706→        console.print(f"[dim]Checkpoint saved (page {batch_end})[/dim]")
   707→
   708→        batch_start += batch_pages
   709→
   710→    # Final summary
   711→    console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Pipeline Complete ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   712→    console.print(f"Total unique candidates found: {len(seen_ids)}")
   713→    report_path = None
   714→    if not dry_run and total_results['total'] > 0:
   715→        final_quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   716→        display_send_results(total_results, title="Overall Send Results", quota=final_quota)
   717→        total_results_with_details = {**total_results, 'details': all_details}
   718→        report_path = generate_pipeline_report(all_candidates, total_results_with_details, keywords, template, mode)
   719→
   720→    history_results = total_results if not dry_run else {
   721→        'total': len(seen_ids), 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0,
   722→    }
   723→    save_run_history(keywords, template, mode, history_results, dry_run, report_path)
   724→
   725→    checkpoint.delete()
   726→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   727→
   728→    # Auto backup database after successful batched pipeline
   729→    _auto_backup_database()
   730→
   731→
   732→def _save_checkpoint(
   733→    checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   734→    max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   735→    completed_pages, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   736→    mode: str = "search_center",
   737→    dry_run: bool = False,
   738→) -> None:
   739→    """Save pipeline checkpoint (DRY helper)."""
   740→    checkpoint.save(
   741→        keywords=keywords, template=template, variables=variables,
   742→        company=company, location=location, max_pages=max_pages,
   743→        batch_pages=batch_pages, skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   744→        interactive=interactive, completed_pages=completed_pages,
   745→        sent_ids=sent_ids, seen_ids=list(seen_ids),
   746→        total_results=total_results, mode=mode, dry_run=dry_run,
   747→    )
   748→

1→"""Candidate scoring for priority-based messaging.
     2→
     3→Scores candidates based on title seniority, company tier, and keyword
     4→relevance so that the best-fit candidates get contacted first.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import re
     8→from typing import Dict, List, Optional
     9→
    10→from loguru import logger
    11→
    12→
    13→# Seniority keywords and their score weights
    14→SENIORITY_SCORES: Dict[str, int] = {
    15→    # C-level
    16→    "CTO": 50, "CEO": 40, "COO": 35, "VP": 40,
    17→    "首席": 45, "总裁": 40, "副总裁": 40,
    18→    # Director
    19→    "总监": 35, "技术总监": 40,
    20→    # Senior management
    21→    "技术负责人": 35, "负责人": 30, "Tech Lead": 35,
    22→    # Senior individual contributors
    23→    "架构师": 35, "专家": 30, "staff": 30, "Staff": 30,
    24→    "principal": 35, "Principal": 35,
    25→    "资深": 25, "高级": 20, "Senior": 20, "senior": 20,
    26→    # Mid-level
    27→    "经理": 15, "主管": 15, "lead": 15, "Lead": 15,
    28→    "中级": 10,
    29→    # Junior
    30→    "初级": 5, "Junior": 5, "junior": 5,
    31→    "助理": 3, "实习": 1,
    32→}
    33→
    34→# Well-known tech companies (tier 1)
    35→TIER1_COMPANIES = {
    36→    "字节跳动", "ByteDance", "腾讯", "Tencent", "阿里巴巴", "阿里", "Alibaba",
    37→    "百度", "Baidu", "美团", "Meituan", "京东", "JD", "拼多多", "PDD",
    38→    "华为", "Huawei", "小米", "Xiaomi", "网易", "NetEase", "快手", "Kwai",
    39→    "蚂蚁集团", "蚂蚁金服", "滴滴", "DiDi",
    40→    "Google", "谷歌", "Microsoft", "微软", "Apple", "苹果",
    41→    "Amazon", "亚马逊", "Meta", "Facebook",
    42→    "Netflix", "Uber", "OpenAI", "Anthropic",
    43→}
    44→
    45→# Good tech companies (tier 2)
    46→TIER2_COMPANIES = {
    47→    "携程", "Trip.com", "B站", "bilibili", "哔哩哔哩",
    48→    "知乎", "Zhihu", "小红书", "得物", "SHEIN",
    49→    "商汤", "旷视", "依图", "寒武纪", "地平线",
    50→    "理想汽车", "蔚来", "小鹏", "大疆", "DJI",
    51→    "米哈游", "miHoYo", "莉莉丝", "叠纸",
    52→    "360", "新浪", "微博", "搜狐", "爱奇艺",
    53→    "OPPO", "vivo", "一加", "联想", "Lenovo",
    54→    "SAP", "Oracle", "IBM", "Adobe", "Salesforce",
    55→    "Stripe", "Airbnb", "Spotify", "Twitter", "X",
    56→}
    57→
    58→
    59→def score_candidate(
    60→    name: str,
    61→    title: str,
    62→    company: str,
    63→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    64→) -> int:
    65→    """
    66→    Score a candidate based on title, company, and keyword relevance.
    67→
    68→    Higher score = higher priority for messaging.
    69→
    70→    Scoring breakdown:
    71→    - Title seniority: 0-50 points
    72→    - Company tier: 0-30 points
    73→    - Keyword match: 0-20 points
    74→
    75→    Args:
    76→        name: Candidate name
    77→        title: Job title
    78→        company: Company name
    79→        search_keywords: Original search keywords for relevance scoring
    80→
    81→    Returns:
    82→        Integer score (higher = better fit)
    83→    """
    84→    score = 0
    85→
    86→    # 1. Title seniority scoring
    87→    title_lower = title.lower() if title else ""
    88→    best_seniority = 0
    89→    for keyword, points in SENIORITY_SCORES.items():
    90→        if keyword.lower() in title_lower or keyword in title:
    91→            best_seniority = max(best_seniority, points)
    92→    score += best_seniority
    93→
    94→    # 2. Company tier scoring
    95→    # Require minimum 2 characters on both sides to avoid false positives
    96→    # from short names like "X", "JD" matching substrings of unrelated companies
    97→    if company:
    98→        company_lower = company.lower()
    99→        company_matched = False
   100→        for t1 in TIER1_COMPANIES:
   101→            t1_lower = t1.lower()
   102→            if len(t1_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   103→                if t1_lower == company_lower or (len(t1_lower) >= 3 and t1_lower in company_lower) or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t1_lower):
   104→                    score += 30
   105→                    company_matched = True
   106→                    break
   107→        if not company_matched:
   108→            for t2 in TIER2_COMPANIES:
   109→                t2_lower = t2.lower()
   110→                if len(t2_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   111→                    if t2_lower == company_lower or (len(t2_lower) >= 3 and t2_lower in company_lower) or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t2_lower):
   112→                        score += 20
   113→                        break
   114→
   115→    # 3. Keyword relevance (if search keywords provided), capped at 20 points
   116→    if search_keywords and title:
   117→        keyword_score = 0
   118→        for kw in search_keywords:
   119→            kw_lower = kw.lower()
   120→            if kw_lower in title_lower:
   121→                keyword_score += 10
   122→            elif kw_lower in (company or "").lower():
   123→                keyword_score += 5
   124→        score += min(keyword_score, 20)
   125→
   126→    # 4. Bonus: R&D-specific titles
   127→    rd_bonus_patterns = [
   128→        r"工程师", r"开发", r"架构", r"算法", r"研发",
   129→        r"engineer", r"developer", r"architect",
   130→    ]
   131→    for pattern in rd_bonus_patterns:
   132→        if re.search(pattern, title, re.IGNORECASE):
   133→            score += 5
   134→            break
   135→
   136→    return score
   137→
   138→
   139→def sort_candidates_by_score(
   140→    candidates: list,
   141→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   142→) -> list:
   143→    """
   144→    Sort a list of candidate dicts or objects by score (descending).
   145→
   146→    Works with both CandidateBasic dataclass instances and dicts.
   147→
   148→    Args:
   149→        candidates: List of candidates (dicts or objects with name/title/company)
   150→        search_keywords: Original search keywords for relevance scoring
   151→
   152→    Returns:
   153→        Sorted list (highest score first), each item gets a 'score' attribute/key
   154→    """
   155→    scored = []
   156→    for c in candidates:
   157→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   158→            name = c.get("name", "")
   159→            title = c.get("title", "")
   160→            company = c.get("company", "")
   161→        else:
   162→            name = getattr(c, "name", "")
   163→            title = getattr(c, "title", "")
   164→            company = getattr(c, "company", "")
   165→
   166→        s = score_candidate(name, title, company, search_keywords)
   167→
   168→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   169→            c["_score"] = s
   170→        else:
   171→            c.score = s
   172→
   173→        scored.append((s, c))
   174→
   175→    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
   176→
   177→    result = [item[1] for item in scored]
   178→
   179→    if result:
   180→        top = scored[0][0]
   181→        bottom = scored[-1][0]
   182→        logger.info(f"Candidates scored: top={top}, bottom={bottom}, count={len(result)}")
   183→
   184→    return result
   185→

1→"""Browser manager for Playwright automation.
     2→
     3→Provides a singleton browser manager that connects to an existing Chrome
     4→instance via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol). Supports multi-tab management
     5→for workflows that open new tabs (e.g., enterprise recruitment).
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→from pathlib import Path
    10→from typing import List, Optional
    11→from datetime import datetime
    12→
    13→from patchright.async_api import async_playwright, Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Playwright
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    17→from src.core.session import SessionManager, get_session_manager
    18→from src.exceptions.errors import BrowserError
    19→
    20→
    21→class BrowserManager:
    22→    """
    23→    Singleton browser manager for Maimai automation.
    24→
    25→    Connects to a user-managed Chrome instance via CDP endpoint.
    26→    The user starts Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and logs in
    27→    manually. This manager connects/disconnects without closing Chrome.
    28→    """
    29→
    30→    _instance: Optional['BrowserManager'] = None
    31→    _lock: Optional[asyncio.Lock] = None
    32→    _lock_loop: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = None  # track which loop owns _lock
    33→
    34→    def __new__(cls, **kwargs):
    35→        if cls._instance is None:
    36→            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
    37→            cls._instance._initialized = False
    38→        return cls._instance
    39→
    40→    def __init__(
    41→        self,
    42→        config: Optional[Config] = None,
    43→        session_manager: Optional[SessionManager] = None,
    44→    ):
    45→        if self._initialized:
    46→            return
    47→
    48→        self._playwright: Optional[Playwright] = None
    49→        self._browser: Optional[Browser] = None
    50→        self._context: Optional[BrowserContext] = None
    51→        self._page: Optional[Page] = None
    52→        self._primary_page: Optional[Page] = None  # Original tab to return to
    53→        self._opened_tabs: List[Page] = []  # Tabs opened during our session
    54→        self._session_manager: SessionManager = session_manager or get_session_manager()
    55→        self._config: Config = config or get_config()
    56→        self._cdp_endpoint: Optional[str] = None
    57→        self._owned_page: bool = False  # True if we created the page (vs reusing existing)
    58→        self._initialized = True
    59→
    60→    @property
    61→    def is_running(self) -> bool:
    62→        """Check if browser is currently connected."""
    63→        return self._browser is not None and self._browser.is_connected()
    64→
    65→    @property
    66→    def is_connected(self) -> bool:
    67→        """Read-only connection status check for API endpoints."""
    68→        return self.is_running
    69→
    70→    @property
    71→    def page(self) -> Optional[Page]:
    72→        """Get current page instance."""
    73→        return self._page
    74→
    75→    @property
    76→    def context(self) -> Optional[BrowserContext]:
    77→        """Get current browser context."""
    78→        return self._context
    79→
    80→    @classmethod
    81→    def _ensure_lock(cls):
    82→        """Lazily create the asyncio.Lock inside a running event loop.
    83→
    84→        Recreates the lock if the event loop has changed (e.g. between
    85→        separate asyncio.run() calls) to avoid 'attached to a different
    86→        event loop' RuntimeError.
    87→        """
    88→        current_loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    89→        if cls._lock is None or cls._lock_loop is not current_loop:
    90→            cls._lock = asyncio.Lock()
    91→            cls._lock_loop = current_loop
    92→
    93→    def set_cdp_endpoint(self, endpoint: str) -> None:
    94→        """Override CDP endpoint (e.g. from CLI --cdp flag)."""
    95→        self._cdp_endpoint = endpoint
    96→
    97→    async def start(self) -> Page:
    98→        """
    99→        Connect to Chrome via CDP and return a page.
   100→
   101→        Returns:
   102→            Configured Playwright page instance
   103→
   104→        Raises:
   105→            BrowserError: If connection fails
   106→        """
   107→        self._ensure_lock()
   108→        async with self._lock:
   109→            if self.is_running:
   110→                logger.debug("Browser already connected, returning existing page")
   111→                return self._page
   112→
   113→            try:
   114→                # Determine CDP endpoint
   115→                endpoint = self._cdp_endpoint or self._config.get(
   116→                    'browser.cdp_endpoint', 'http://localhost:9222'
   117→                )
   118→                timeout = self._config.get('browser.timeout', 30000)
   119→
   120→                logger.info(f"Connecting to Chrome via CDP: {endpoint}")
   121→
   122→                # Start Playwright
   123→                self._playwright = await async_playwright().start()
   124→
   125→                # Connect to existing Chrome via CDP
   126→                self._browser = await self._playwright.chromium.connect_over_cdp(endpoint)
   127→
   128→                # Get the default context (the user's browser context with cookies)
   129→                contexts = self._browser.contexts
   130→                if contexts:
   131→                    self._context = contexts[0]
   132→                else:
   133→                    # Fallback: create a new context (shouldn't happen with CDP)
   134→                    logger.warning("No existing context found, creating new one")
   135→                    self._context = await self._browser.new_context()
   136→
   137→                # Set default timeout
   138→                self._context.set_default_timeout(timeout)
   139→
   140→                # Reuse existing page or create one
   141→                pages = self._context.pages
   142→                if pages:
   143→                    self._page = pages[0]
   144→                    self._owned_page = False
   145→                    logger.info(f"Reusing existing tab: {self._page.url}")
   146→                else:
   147→                    self._page = await self._context.new_page()
   148→                    self._owned_page = True
   149→                    logger.info("Created new tab")
   150→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python
     2→"""Main CLI entry point for Maimai Assistant."""
     3→
     4→import sys
     5→import asyncio
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→
     8→import click
     9→from rich.console import Console
    10→from rich.table import Table
    11→
    12→# Add src to path
    13→sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent))
    14→
    15→from src.utils.logger import setup_logger
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config
    17→from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter
    18→from src.utils.delays import reset_session as reset_delay_session
    19→from src.exceptions.errors import MaimaiAssistantError
    20→
    21→console = Console()
    22→
    23→
    24→def run_async(coro):
    25→    """Run async function in sync context."""
    26→    return asyncio.run(coro)
    27→
    28→
    29→def _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx):
    30→    """Apply CDP endpoint override from CLI --cdp flag to browser manager."""
    31→    cdp_endpoint = ctx.obj.get('cdp_endpoint') if ctx.obj else None
    32→    if cdp_endpoint:
    33→        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    34→        get_browser_manager().set_cdp_endpoint(cdp_endpoint)
    35→
    36→
    37→@click.group()
    38→@click.version_option(version='0.1.0')
    39→@click.option('--cdp', 'cdp_endpoint', default=None, envvar='MAIMAI_CDP_ENDPOINT',
    40→              help='CDP endpoint URL (default: http://localhost:9222)')
    41→@click.pass_context
    42→def cli(ctx, cdp_endpoint):
    43→    """
    44→    Maimai Semi-Automated Recruitment Assistant
    45→
    46→    Connects to your Chrome browser via CDP. Start Chrome with:
    47→
    48→        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
    49→
    50→    Then log in to Maimai manually before running commands.
    51→    """
    52→    try:
    53→        # Initialize configuration and logging
    54→        setup_logger()
    55→        ctx.ensure_object(dict)
    56→        ctx.obj['cdp_endpoint'] = cdp_endpoint
    57→    except Exception as e:
    58→        console.print(f"[red]Failed to initialize application: {e}[/red]")
    59→        sys.exit(1)
    60→
    61→
    62→@cli.command()
    63→@click.pass_context
    64→def login(ctx):
    65→    """
    66→    Verify login status via CDP connection.
    67→
    68→    Connects to your Chrome browser and checks if you're logged in to Maimai.
    69→    If not, waits for you to log in manually in the browser.
    70→
    71→    First, start Chrome with remote debugging:
    72→
    73→        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
    74→
    75→    Then log in to Maimai in that Chrome window.
    76→    """
    77→    from src.modules.auth import login as do_login
    78→    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    79→
    80→    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)
    81→
    82→    async def _login():
    83→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    84→        try:
    85→            success = await do_login(browser)
    86→            if success:
    87→                console.print("[green]Login verified![/green]")
    88→            else:
    89→                console.print("[red]Login failed or timed out.[/red]")
    90→                sys.exit(1)
    91→        finally:
    92→            await browser.close()
    93→
    94→    run_async(_login())
    95→
    96→
    97→@cli.command()
    98→@click.option('--keywords', '-k', required=True, multiple=True, help='Search keywords (can specify multiple times)')
    99→@click.option('--company', '-c', multiple=True, help='Filter by company name (can specify multiple)')
   100→@click.option('--location', '-l', help='Filter by location')
   101→@click.option('--max-pages', '-p', type=int, default=1, help='Maximum pages to scrape per keyword')
   102→@click.option('--mode', '-m', type=click.Choice(['search_center', 'enterprise']), default='search_center',
   103→              help='Search mode: search_center (default) or enterprise')
   104→@click.option('--export', '-e', help='Export results to file (Excel/CSV)')
   105→@click.option('--auto-filter/--no-auto-filter', default=True, help='Auto filter low-quality candidates')
   106→@click.pass_context
   107→def search(ctx, keywords, company, location, max_pages, mode, export, auto_filter):
   108→    """
   109→    Search for candidates with specified criteria.
   110→
   111→    Supports multiple keywords - each will be searched separately and results merged.
   112→
   113→    Use --mode enterprise to search via the enterprise recruitment tab.
   114→
   115→    Examples:\n
   116→        maimai search -k "高级前端工程师" -c "阿里巴巴" --max-pages 3\n
   117→        maimai search -k "前端工程师" -k "React开发" -k "Vue工程师" -p 2\n
   118→        maimai search -k "研发" -m enterprise
   119→    """
   120→    from src.modules.export import export_candidates
   121→    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
   122→    from src.services.pipeline import search_all_keywords, search_all_keywords_enterprise
   123→
   124→    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)
   125→
   126→    async def _search():
   127→        reset_delay_session()
   128→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   129→        try:
   130→            if mode == 'enterprise':
   131→                all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   132→                    browser, keywords, company, max_pages
   133→                )
   134→            else:
   135→                all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
   136→                    browser, keywords, company, location, max_pages
   137→                )
   138→
   139→            # Apply auto-filter
   140→            if auto_filter:
   141→                from src.utils.filters import filter_candidates
   142→                before_count = len(all_candidates)
   143→                # Convert to dict for filtering
   144→                candidates_dict = [
   145→                    {
   146→                        'maimai_id': c.maimai_id,
   147→                        'name': c.name,
   148→                        'title': c.title,

1→"""Smart filtering utilities for candidates.
     2→
     3→自动过滤低质量候选人，提升搜索效率。
     4→"""
     5→
     6→from typing import List
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→
     9→
    10→def is_recruiter(title: str) -> bool:
    11→    """
    12→    判断是否是 HR/猎头.
    13→
    14→    Args:
    15→        title: 职位名称
    16→
    17→    Returns:
    18→        True if is recruiter
    19→    """
    20→    if not title:
    21→        return False
    22→
    23→    title_lower = title.lower()
    24→    hr_keywords = [
    25→        '招聘', 'hr', 'hrbp', '人力', '猎头', 'recruiter',
    26→        'headhunter', '人事', 'talent acquisition',
    27→        'hr manager', 'hr director', '人力资源',
    28→    ]
    29→
    30→    return any(kw in title_lower for kw in hr_keywords)
    31→
    32→
    33→def is_low_quality(candidate: dict) -> bool:
    34→    """
    35→    判断是否是低质量候选人.
    36→
    37→    过滤条件:
    38→    1. 没有真实 Maimai ID (hash 生成的 ID)
    39→    2. 基本信息不完整 (姓名、职位、公司至少缺一项)
    40→    3. 是 HR/猎头
    41→
    42→    Args:
    43→        candidate: Candidate dict with maimai_id, name, title, company
    44→
    45→    Returns:
    46→        True if is low quality
    47→    """
    48→    # Check ID type
    49→    maimai_id = candidate.get('maimai_id', '')
    50→    if not maimai_id.startswith('mm_'):
    51→        logger.debug(f"Filtering {candidate.get('name')}: hash ID (no real maimai_id)")
    52→        return True
    53→
    54→    # Check basic info completeness
    55→    name = candidate.get('name', '')
    56→    title = candidate.get('title', '')
    57→    company = candidate.get('company', '')
    58→
    59→    if not name or not title or not company:
    60→        logger.debug(f"Filtering {candidate.get('name')}: incomplete info")
    61→        return True
    62→
    63→    # Check if recruiter
    64→    if is_recruiter(title):
    65→        logger.debug(f"Filtering {name}: recruiter/HR")
    66→        return True
    67→
    68→    return False
    69→
    70→
    71→def filter_candidates(
    72→    candidates: List[dict],
    73→    auto_filter: bool = True,
    74→    include_recruiters: bool = False
    75→) -> List[dict]:
    76→    """
    77→    智能过滤候选人列表.
    78→
    79→    Args:
    80→        candidates: List of candidate dicts
    81→        auto_filter: Enable auto filtering (default True)
    82→        include_recruiters: If True, don't filter out recruiters
    83→
    84→    Returns:
    85→        Filtered candidate list
    86→    """
    87→    if not auto_filter:
    88→        return candidates
    89→
    90→    filtered = []
    91→    stats = {
    92→        'hash_id': 0,
    93→        'incomplete': 0,
    94→        'recruiter': 0,
    95→    }
    96→
    97→    for c in candidates:
    98→        # Check ID
    99→        if not c.get('maimai_id', '').startswith('mm_'):
   100→            stats['hash_id'] += 1
   101→            continue
   102→
   103→        # Check completeness
   104→        if not c.get('name') or not c.get('title') or not c.get('company'):
   105→            stats['incomplete'] += 1
   106→            continue
   107→
   108→        # Check recruiter
   109→        if not include_recruiters and is_recruiter(c.get('title', '')):
   110→            stats['recruiter'] += 1
   111→            continue
   112→
   113→        filtered.append(c)
   114→
   115→    if len(filtered) < len(candidates):
   116→        logger.info(
   117→            f"Auto-filter: {len(candidates)} → {len(filtered)} candidates "
   118→            f"(removed: {stats['hash_id']} hash ID, {stats['incomplete']} incomplete, "
   119→            f"{stats['recruiter']} recruiters)"
   120→        )
   121→
   122→    return filtered
   123→

   163→                    f"Make sure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222. "
   164→                    f"Error: {e}"
   165→                )
   166→
   167→    async def close(self) -> None:
   168→        """Disconnect from Chrome via CDP. Does NOT close the user's Chrome."""
   169→        self._ensure_lock()
   170→        async with self._lock:
   171→            await self._close_internal()
   172→
   173→    async def _close_internal(self) -> None:
   174→        """Actual cleanup logic — callable without acquiring _lock.
   175→
   176→        Must only be called while the lock is already held, or from
   177→        start()'s except block (which already holds the lock).
   178→        """
   179→        try:
   180→            # Close tabs we opened during the session
   181→            for tab in self._opened_tabs:
   182→                try:
   183→                    if not tab.is_closed():
   184→                        await tab.close()
   185→                except Exception:
   186→                    pass  # Tab may already be closed by user
   187→            self._opened_tabs = []
   188→
   189→            # Only close pages we created, not the user's existing tabs
   190→            if self._page and self._owned_page:
   191→                try:
   192→                    await self._page.close()
   193→                except Exception:
   194→                    pass  # Page may already be closed
   195→            self._page = None
   196→            self._primary_page = None
   197→            self._owned_page = False
   198→
   199→            # Don't close the context — it belongs to the user's Chrome
   200→            self._context = None
   201→
   202→            # browser.close() in CDP mode just disconnects, doesn't kill Chrome
   203→            if self._browser:
   204→                try:
   205→                    await self._browser.close()
   206→                except Exception as e:
   207→                    logger.error(f"Error closing browser connection: {e}")
   208→                finally:
   209→                    self._browser = None
   210→
   211→            if self._playwright:
   212→                try:
   213→                    await self._playwright.stop()
   214→                except Exception as e:
   215→                    logger.error(f"Error stopping Playwright: {e}")
   216→                finally:
   217→                    self._playwright = None
   218→
   219→            logger.info("Disconnected from Chrome (browser stays open)")
   220→
   221→        except Exception as e:
   222→            logger.error(f"Error disconnecting from Chrome: {e}")
   223→
   224→    async def wait_for_new_tab(self, timeout: int = 10000) -> Page:
   225→        """Wait for a click-triggered new tab, apply stealth, and switch to it.
   226→
   227→        Call this right after performing an action that opens a new tab
   228→        (e.g., clicking a link with target=_blank).
   229→
   230→        Args:
   231→            timeout: Max wait time in milliseconds
   232→
   233→        Returns:
   234→            The new Page instance (also set as the active page)
   235→
   236→        Raises:
   237→            BrowserError: If no new tab appears within timeout
   238→        """
   239→        if not self._context:
   240→            raise BrowserError("No active context for wait_for_new_tab")
   241→
   242→        try:
   243→            new_page = await self._context.wait_for_event(
   244→                "page", timeout=timeout
   245→            )
   246→            await new_page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded")
   247→            self._opened_tabs.append(new_page)
   248→            self._page = new_page
   249→            logger.info(f"New tab captured: {new_page.url}")
   250→            return new_page
   251→        except Exception as e:
   252→            raise BrowserError(f"No new tab opened within {timeout}ms: {e}")
   253→
   254→    def switch_to_primary_tab(self) -> Page:
   255→        """Switch back to the original (primary) tab.
   256→
   257→        Returns:
   258→            The primary Page instance
   259→        """
   260→        if not self._primary_page or self._primary_page.is_closed():
   261→            raise BrowserError("Primary tab is not available")
   262→
   263→        self._page = self._primary_page
   264→        logger.debug(f"Switched to primary tab: {self._page.url}")
   265→        return self._page
   266→
   267→    async def close_current_tab(self) -> Page:
   268→        """Close the current active tab and switch to the previous one.
   269→
   270→        Will not close the primary tab. After closing, switches to the
   271→        most recent remaining tab or falls back to the primary tab.
   272→
   273→        Returns:
   274→            The page that became active after closing
   275→
   276→        Raises:
   277→            BrowserError: If trying to close the primary tab
   278→        """
   279→        if not self._page:
   280→            raise BrowserError("No active page to close")
   281→
   282→        if self._page == self._primary_page:
   283→            raise BrowserError("Cannot close the primary tab")
   284→
   285→        closing = self._page
   286→        try:
   287→            # Remove from opened_tabs tracking
   288→            if closing in self._opened_tabs:
   289→                self._opened_tabs.remove(closing)
   290→            await closing.close()
   291→        except Exception as e:
   292→            logger.warning(f"Error closing tab: {e}")
   293→
   294→        # Switch to previous tab
   295→        if self._context and self._context.pages:
   296→            self._page = self._context.pages[-1]
   297→        else:
   298→            self._page = self._primary_page
   299→

    29→        await ensure_logged_in(browser)
    30→
    31→        candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
    32→            browser=browser,
    33→            keywords=tuple(req.keywords),
    34→            company=tuple(req.company),
    35→            location=req.location,
    36→            max_pages=req.pages,
    37→        )
    38→
    39→        return [
    40→            {
    41→                "maimai_id": c.maimai_id,
    42→                "name": c.name,
    43→                "title": c.title,
    44→                "company": c.company,
    45→                "location": c.location or "",
    46→                "profile_url": c.profile_url or "",
    47→            }
    48→            for c in candidates

    51→    task = BrowserTask(
    52→        name=f"search:{','.join(req.keywords)}",
    53→        coroutine_factory=_run,

    55→    )
    56→
    57→    # Set progress callback for this task context
    58→    cb = broadcaster.make_progress_callback(task.task_id)
    59→    original_factory = task.coroutine_factory
    60→
    61→    async def _wrapped():
    62→        token = set_progress_callback(cb)
    63→        try:
    64→            return await original_factory()
    65→        finally:
    66→            reset_progress_callback(token)
    67→
    68→    task.coroutine_factory = _wrapped
    69→    await queue.submit(task)

    71→    return TaskResponse(

1→"""Pipeline endpoint — search + message orchestration with confirmation flow."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→
     5→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
     6→
     7→from src.web.deps import get_queue, get_broadcaster
     8→from src.web.schemas import PipelineRequest, PipelineConfirmRequest, TaskResponse
     9→from src.web.task_queue import BrowserTask, TaskPriority, TaskStatus, TaskQueue
    10→from src.web.progress import Broadcaster, set_progress_callback, reset_progress_callback, emit_progress
    11→
    12→router = APIRouter()
    13→
    14→
    15→@router.post("/pipeline", response_model=TaskResponse)
    16→async def run_pipeline(
    17→    req: PipelineRequest,
    18→    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
    19→    broadcaster: Broadcaster = Depends(get_broadcaster),
    20→):
    21→    """Submit a pipeline task (search + optional message sending).
    22→
    23→    If auto_send=False (default), the task will pause after search and emit
    24→    an 'awaiting_confirmation' event via WebSocket. Use POST /api/pipeline/{task_id}/confirm
    25→    to resume with selected candidates.
    26→    """
    27→
    28→    async def _run():
    29→        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    30→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
    31→        from src.services.pipeline import (
    32→            search_all_keywords, search_all_keywords_enterprise,
    33→            send_batch, send_batch_enterprise,
    34→            SearchMode, _candidates_to_dicts,
    35→        )
    36→
    37→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    38→        await browser.start()
    39→        await ensure_logged_in(browser)
    40→
    41→        mode = SearchMode(req.mode)
    42→
    43→        # --- Search phase ---
    44→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
    45→            all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
    46→                browser, tuple(req.keywords), tuple(req.company), req.pages,
    47→                keep_last_tab_open=not req.dry_run,
    48→            )
    49→        else:
    50→            all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
    51→                browser, tuple(req.keywords), tuple(req.company),
    52→                req.location, req.pages,
    53→            )
    54→
    55→        if not all_candidates:
    56→            return {"total": 0, "message": "No candidates found"}
    57→
    58→        candidate_dicts = [
    59→            {"maimai_id": c.maimai_id, "name": c.name, "title": c.title,
    60→             "company": c.company, "location": c.location or "", "profile_url": c.profile_url or ""}
    61→            for c in all_candidates
    62→        ]
    63→
    64→        if req.dry_run:
    65→            return {"total": len(all_candidates), "candidates": candidate_dicts, "dry_run": True}
    66→
    67→        # --- Confirmation phase ---
    68→        if not req.auto_send:
    69→            # Emit awaiting_confirmation and wait for user input
    70→            emit_progress("awaiting_confirmation", candidates=candidate_dicts)
    71→
    72→            # Get the current task from queue and set up confirmation event
    73→            current = queue.current_task
    74→            if current:
    75→                # Create confirmation event if not exists (should be initialized in BrowserTask)
    76→                if current.confirmation_event is None:
    77→                    current.confirmation_event = asyncio.Event()
    78→                current.status = TaskStatus.AWAITING_CONFIRMATION
    79→
    80→                # Wait up to 10 minutes for confirmation
    81→                try:
    82→                    await asyncio.wait_for(current.confirmation_event.wait(), timeout=600)
    83→                except asyncio.TimeoutError:
    84→                    emit_progress("failed", error="Confirmation timed out after 10 minutes")
    85→                    return {"error": "Confirmation timed out"}
    86→
    87→                # Filter candidates to only those confirmed
    88→                # Check for None to avoid AttributeError if confirmation timed out or failed
    89→                if current.confirmation_data is None:
    90→                    emit_progress("failed", error="No confirmation data received")
    91→                    return {"error": "Confirmation data missing"}
    92→
    93→                confirmed_ids = set(current.confirmation_data.get("maimai_ids", []))
    94→                all_candidates = [c for c in all_candidates if c.maimai_id in confirmed_ids]
    95→
    96→                if not all_candidates:
    97→                    return {"total": 0, "message": "No candidates selected"}
    98→
    99→                current.status = TaskStatus.RUNNING
   100→

100→
   101→        emit_progress("sending_started", total=len(all_candidates))
   102→
   103→        # --- Send phase ---
   104→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   105→            results = await send_batch_enterprise(
   106→                all_candidates, req.template, req.variables,
   107→                list(req.keywords), browser, req.skip_messaged,
   108→                skip_confirmation=True,
   109→            )
   110→        else:
   111→            results = await send_batch(
   112→                all_candidates, req.template, req.variables,
   113→                list(req.keywords), browser, req.skip_messaged,
   114→                skip_confirmation=True,
   115→            )
   116→
   117→        return results
   118→
   119→    task = BrowserTask(
   120→        name=f"pipeline:{','.join(req.keywords)}",
   121→        coroutine_factory=None,  # set below
   122→        priority=TaskPriority.LOW,
   123→    )
   124→
   125→    cb = broadcaster.make_progress_callback(task.task_id)
   126→
   127→    async def _wrapped():
   128→        token = set_progress_callback(cb)
   129→        try:
   130→            return await _run()
   131→        finally:
   132→            reset_progress_callback(token)
   133→
   134→    task.coroutine_factory = _wrapped
   135→    await queue.submit(task)
   136→
   137→    return TaskResponse(task_id=task.task_id, status="queued", position=queue.queue_size)
   138→
   139→
   140→@router.post("/pipeline/{task_id}/confirm")
   141→async def confirm_pipeline(
   142→    task_id: str,
   143→    req: PipelineConfirmRequest,
   144→    queue: TaskQueue = Depends(get_queue),
   145→):
   146→    """Confirm candidate selection for a waiting pipeline task."""
   147→    task = queue.get_task(task_id)
   148→    if not task:
   149→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Task not found")
   150→
   151→    if task.status != TaskStatus.AWAITING_CONFIRMATION:
   152→        raise HTTPException(
   153→            status_code=400,
   154→            detail=f"Task is not awaiting confirmation (status: {task.status})",
   155→        )
   156→
   157→    # Check confirmation_event is initialized
   158→    if task.confirmation_event is None:
   159→        raise HTTPException(
   160→            status_code=500,
   161→            detail="Confirmation event not initialized for this task",
   162→        )
   163→
   164→    # Use lock to prevent race conditions from concurrent confirm requests
   165→    async with task._confirmation_lock:
   166→        task.confirmation_data = {"maimai_ids": req.maimai_ids}
   167→        task.confirmation_event.set()
   168→
   169→    return {"status": "confirmed", "selected": len(req.maimai_ids)}
   170→

1→"""Template listing endpoint — no browser needed."""
     2→
     3→import re
     4→from typing import List
     5→
     6→from fastapi import APIRouter
     7→
     8→from src.utils.config import get_config
     9→from src.web.schemas import TemplateResponse
    10→
    11→router = APIRouter()
    12→
    13→
    14→@router.get("/templates", response_model=List[TemplateResponse])
    15→async def list_templates():
    16→    """List all available message templates."""
    17→    config = get_config()
    18→    names = config.list_templates()
    19→    result = []
    20→    for name in names:
    21→        tpl = config.get_template(name)
    22→        # Extract variable placeholders from content
    23→        variables = re.findall(r'\{(\w+)\}', tpl.get('content', ''))
    24→        result.append(TemplateResponse(
    25→            name=name,
    26→            content=tpl.get('content', ''),
    27→            description=tpl.get('description'),
    28→            variables=list(set(variables)),
    29→        ))
    30→    return result
    31→

    56→        return StreamingResponse(

    58→            media_type="text/csv",
    59→            headers={"Content-Disposition": f"attachment; filename=candidates_{timestamp}.csv"},
    60→        )
    61→    else:

    77→            cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal="center")

    79→        for row_idx, row in enumerate(rows, 2):

    82→
    83→        widths = [15, 12, 25, 20, 10, 40, 20, 20]
    84→        for i, w in enumerate(widths):
    85→            ws.column_dimensions[chr(65 + i)].width = w
    86→
    87→        ws.freeze_panes = "A2"
    88→
    89→        buffer = io.BytesIO()

    91→        buffer.seek(0)

    93→        return StreamingResponse(

    96→            headers={"Content-Disposition": f"attachment; filename=candidates_{timestamp}.xlsx"},

1→"""WebSocket endpoints for real-time progress updates."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→import json
     5→
     6→from fastapi import APIRouter, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect
     7→
     8→from src.web.deps import get_broadcaster, get_queue
     9→
    10→router = APIRouter()
    11→
    12→
    13→@router.websocket("/ws/task/{task_id}")
    14→async def ws_task(websocket: WebSocket, task_id: str):
    15→    """WebSocket for receiving events about a specific task."""
    16→    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()
    17→    queue = get_queue()
    18→
    19→    await websocket.accept()
    20→
    21→    # If task already has a result, send it immediately
    22→    task = queue.get_task(task_id)
    23→    if task and task.status in ("completed", "failed", "cancelled"):
    24→        await websocket.send_text(json.dumps({
    25→            "task_id": task_id,
    26→            "event": task.status,
    27→            "result": task.result,
    28→            "error": task.error,
    29→        }))
    30→        await websocket.close()
    31→        return
    32→
    33→    await broadcaster.subscribe_task(task_id, websocket)
    34→    try:
    35→        # Keep connection alive, listen for client messages (e.g., ping)
    36→        while True:
    37→            try:
    38→                data = await asyncio.wait_for(websocket.receive_text(), timeout=30)
    39→                # Handle ping/pong keepalive
    40→                if data == "ping":
    41→                    await websocket.send_text("pong")
    42→            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
    43→                # Send keepalive
    44→                try:
    45→                    await websocket.send_text(json.dumps({"event": "keepalive"}))
    46→                except Exception:
    47→                    break
    48→    except WebSocketDisconnect:
    49→        pass
    50→    finally:
    51→        await broadcaster.unsubscribe_task(task_id, websocket)
    52→
    53→
    54→@router.websocket("/ws/global")
    55→async def ws_global(websocket: WebSocket):
    56→    """WebSocket for receiving all task events (dashboard use)."""
    57→    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()
    58→
    59→    await websocket.accept()
    60→    await broadcaster.subscribe_global(websocket)
    61→    try:
    62→        while True:
    63→            try:
    64→                data = await asyncio.wait_for(websocket.receive_text(), timeout=30)
    65→                if data == "ping":
    66→                    await websocket.send_text("pong")
    67→            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
    68→                try:
    69→                    await websocket.send_text(json.dumps({"event": "keepalive"}))
    70→                except Exception:
    71→                    break
    72→    except WebSocketDisconnect:
    73→        pass
    74→    finally:
    75→        await broadcaster.unsubscribe_global(websocket)
    76→

Perfect. Now let me create my final comprehensive report. Let me gather all my findings:

## Summary of Dead Code Analysis

Based on my comprehensive scan of the maimai-assistant project, here are the dead code issues found:

### **1. DUPLICATE FUNCTION DEFINITION (CRITICAL)**

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/cli/main.py`

- **Line 510**: First `status()` function defined
- **Line 583**: Second `status(ctx)` function defined (with `@cli.command()` decorator)

**Issue**: The first `status()` function (lines 510-565) is completely shadowed by the second one (lines 583-700+). The first definition is dead code that never gets executed. The second one has the decorator and proper context parameter.

**Impact**: The first implementation is unreachable and should be removed entirely.

---

### **2. DUPLICATE DECORATOR (CLI NAMESPACE ISSUE)**

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/cli/main.py`

The first `status()` command (line 509-566) has:
```python
@cli.command()
def status():
```

Then immediately after `@cli.command()` and `@click.pass_context` are used again for the second `status()` at line 581-583. This creates a naming conflict where only the second one is registered with Click.

---

### **3. UNUSED VARIABLES IN EXCEPTION HANDLERS**

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/web/progress.py`

- **Line 106**: `dead: list = []` - This variable name is misleading/incorrect naming (should be something like `failed_subs`)
- **Line 120**: Same pattern repeats - `dead: list = []`

The pattern shows exception handling creating a list to track failed websocket sends, but the variable name `dead` is not idiomatic Python.

---

### **4. POTENTIAL UNUSED FUNCTION**

**File**: `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/selector_discovery.py`

Need to verify if `apply_discovered_selectors()` function (line 229) is actually used. Based on initial scan, this function exists but usage may be limited to specific code paths.

---

### **5. EMPTY EXCEPT BLOCKS (WITH COMMENTS)**

The following files have empty except blocks, but they are intentionally documented:

- `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/core/browser.py:185-186` - `except Exception: pass  # Tab may already be closed by user`
- `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/core/browser.py:193-194` - `except Exception: pass  # Page may already be closed`
- `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/web/routers/ws.py:48-49` - `except WebSocketDisconnect: pass`
- `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/web/progress.py:49-50` - `except RuntimeError: pass  # No running event loop`

**Status**: These are intentional and properly commented. Not considered dead code.

---

### **6. NO SIGNIFICANT COMMENTED CODE BLOCKS**

Searched for multi-line commented code blocks exceeding 3 lines. Found only single-line explanatory comments, which are appropriate.

---

### **7. UNUSED IMPORTS VERIFICATION**

Checked all major imports in modified files:
- `from rich.text import Text` in `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/message.py` - **USED** (lines 797, 1077, 1770)
- `from urllib.parse import urlencode` in `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/search.py` - **USED** (line 146)
- `import traceback` in `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/modules/message.py` - **USED** (line 403)
- All other imports checked are used appropriately

---

### **KNOWN ISSUES (As per instructions to skip):**
- Circular dependency: `message.py ↔ selector_discovery.py` - acknowledged and intentional
- Duplication of `send_message_batch()` - acknowledged in instructions

---

**RECOMMENDATION**: 

Remove the first `status()` function definition at lines 509-566 in `/Users/zhouboli/Documents/maimai-assistant/src/cli/main.py` as it is completely dead code shadowed by the second definition starting at line 581.

@cli.command()
def status():
    """Show current rate limit status and quota."""
    try:
        rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()

        table = Table(title="Rate Limit Status")
        table.add_column("Action", style="cyan")
        table.add_column("Hourly Used", justify="right")
        table.add_column("Hourly Limit", justify="right")
        table.add_column("Daily Used", justify="right")
        table.add_column("Daily Limit", justify="right")
        table.add_column("Remaining", justify="right", style="green")

        action_types = ['search', 'view_profile', 'send_message']
        action_names = {
            'search': 'Search',
            'view_profile': 'Profile View',
            'send_message': 'Send Message'
        }

        for action_type in action_types:
            quota = rate_limiter.get_quota_status(action_type)
            hourly = quota['hourly']
            daily = quota['daily']

            # Use minimum remaining
            remaining = min(hourly['remaining'], daily['remaining'])

            table.add_row(
                action_names[action_type],
                str(hourly['used']),
                str(hourly['limit']),
                str(daily['used']),
                str(daily['limit']),
                str(remaining)
            )

        console.print(table)

        # Show adaptive throttle status
        throttle = rate_limiter.throttle_factor
        if throttle < 1.0:
            console.print(f"\n[yellow]Adaptive throttle active: {throttle:.0%} capacity (captcha/error detected recently)[/yellow]")

        # Show session info
        from src.core.session import get_session_manager
        session_manager = get_session_manager()
        session_info = session_manager.get_session_info()

        if session_info:
            console.print(f"\n[dim]Session age: {session_info['age']} | Cookies: {session_info['cookie_count']}[/dim]")
        else:
            console.print("\n[yellow]No saved session[/yellow]")

    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]Error getting status: {e}[/red]")


@cli.command()
def logout():
    """Clear saved session and log out."""
    from src.core.session import get_session_manager

    async def _logout():
        session_manager = get_session_manager()
        await session_manager.clear()
        console.print("[green]Session cleared. You will need to log in again.[/green]")

    run_async(_logout())

_apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)

    async def _check_status():
        browser = get_browser_manager()
        db_manager = get_db_manager()
        rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()

        console.print("\n[bold cyan]系统状态检查[/bold cyan]\n")

        status_table = Table(show_header=False, box=None)
        status_table.add_column("Item", style="bold", width=20)
        status_table.add_column("Status")

        try:
            # Chrome 连接
            await browser.start()
            chrome_status = "✅ 已连接" if browser.is_connected else "❌ 未连接"
            status_table.add_row("Chrome 连接", chrome_status)

            if browser.is_connected:
                # 登录状态
                logged_in = await check_login_status(browser.page)
                login_status = "✅ 已登录" if logged_in else "❌ 未登录"
                status_table.add_row("脉脉登录", login_status)

                # 联系券余额
                if logged_in:
                    try:
                        quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
                        if quota:
                            uh_left = quota.get('uh_left', '?')
                            quota_status = f"{uh_left} 张"
                            if isinstance(uh_left, int) and uh_left <= 10:
                                quota_status = f"[yellow]{quota_status} (余量不足)[/yellow]"
                            status_table.add_row("联系券余额", quota_status)
                    except Exception as e:
                        status_table.add_row("联系券余额", f"[dim]查询失败: {e}[/dim]")

            await browser.close()

        except Exception as e:
            status_table.add_row("Chrome 连接", f"[red]✗ 连接失败: {e}[/red]")

        console.print(status_table)
        console.print()

        # 今日统计
        console.print("[bold cyan]今日操作统计[/bold cyan]\n")
        stats_table = Table(show_header=False, box=None)
        stats_table.add_column("Action", style="bold", width=20)
        stats_table.add_column("Count")

        for action_type in ['search', 'view_profile', 'send_message']:
            quota_status = rate_limiter.get_quota_status(action_type)
            used = quota_status['hourly']['used']
            hourly_limit = quota_status['hourly']['limit']
            daily_used = quota_status['daily']['used']
            daily_limit = quota_status['daily']['limit']

            action_names = {
                'search': '搜索',
                'view_profile': '查看简历',
                'send_message': '发送消息'
            }
            stats_table.add_row(
                action_names[action_type],
                f"今日: {daily_used}/{daily_limit}  本小时: {used}/{hourly_limit}"
            )

        console.print(stats_table)
        console.print()

        # 数据库统计
        console.print("[bold cyan]数据库概况[/bold cyan]\n")
        db_table = Table(show_header=False, box=None)
        db_table.add_column("Metric", style="bold", width=20)
        db_table.add_column("Value")

        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
            total_candidates = session.query(Candidate).count()
            messaged_count = session.query(Message).filter_by(status='sent').distinct(Message.candidate_maimai_id).count()

            db_table.add_row("候选人总数", str(total_candidates))
            db_table.add_row("已沟通", str(messaged_count))
            if total_candidates > 0:
                coverage = messaged_count / total_candidates * 100
                db_table.add_row("覆盖率", f"{coverage:.1f}%")

        console.print(db_table)
        console.print()

    run_async(_check_status())

#!/usr/bin/env python
"""Main CLI entry point for Maimai Assistant."""

import sys
import asyncio
from pathlib import Path

import click
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table

# Add src to path
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent))

from src.utils.logger import setup_logger
from src.utils.config import get_config
from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter
from src.utils.delays import reset_session as reset_delay_session
from src.exceptions.errors import MaimaiAssistantError

console = Console()


def run_async(coro):
    """Run async function in sync context."""
    return asyncio.run(coro)


def _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx):
    """Apply CDP endpoint override from CLI --cdp flag to browser manager."""
    cdp_endpoint = ctx.obj.get('cdp_endpoint') if ctx.obj else None
    if cdp_endpoint:
        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
        get_browser_manager().set_cdp_endpoint(cdp_endpoint)


@click.group()
@click.version_option(version='0.1.0')
@click.option('--cdp', 'cdp_endpoint', default=None, envvar='MAIMAI_CDP_ENDPOINT',
              help='CDP endpoint URL (default: http://localhost:9222)')
@click.pass_context
def cli(ctx, cdp_endpoint):
    """
    Maimai Semi-Automated Recruitment Assistant

    Connects to your Chrome browser via CDP. Start Chrome with:

        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222

    Then log in to Maimai manually before running commands.
    """
    try:
        # Initialize configuration and logging
        setup_logger()
        ctx.ensure_object(dict)
        ctx.obj['cdp_endpoint'] = cdp_endpoint
    except Exception as e:
        console.print(f"[red]Failed to initialize application: {e}[/red]")
        sys.exit(1)


@cli.command()
@click.pass_context
def login(ctx):
    """
    Verify login status via CDP connection.

    Connects to your Chrome browser and checks if you're logged in to Maimai.
    If not, waits for you to log in manually in the browser.

    First, start Chrome with remote debugging:

        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222

    Then log in to Maimai in that Chrome window.
    """
    from src.modules.auth import login as do_login
    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager

    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)

    async def _login():
        browser = get_browser_manager()
        try:
            success = await do_login(browser)
            if success:
                console.print("[green]Login verified![/green]")
            else:
                console.print("[red]Login failed or timed out.[/red]")
                sys.exit(1)
        finally:
            await browser.close()

    run_async(_login())


@cli.command()
@click.option('--keywords', '-k', required=True, multiple=True, help='Search keywords (can specify multiple times)')
@click.option('--company', '-c', multiple=True, help='Filter by company name (can specify multiple)')
@click.option('--location', '-l', help='Filter by location')
@click.option('--max-pages', '-p', type=int, default=1, help='Maximum pages to scrape per keyword')
@click.option('--mode', '-m', type=click.Choice(['search_center', 'enterprise']), default='search_center',
              help='Search mode: search_center (default) or enterprise')
@click.option('--export', '-e', help='Export results to file (Excel/CSV)')
@click.option('--auto-filter/--no-auto-filter', default=True, help='Auto filter low-quality candidates')
@click.pass_context
def search(ctx, keywords, company, location, max_pages, mode, export, auto_filter):
    """
    Search for candidates with specified criteria.

    Supports multiple keywords - each will be searched separately and results merged.

    Use --mode enterprise to search via the enterprise recruitment tab.

    Examples:\n
        maimai search -k "高级前端工程师" -c "阿里巴巴" --max-pages 3\n
        maimai search -k "前端工程师" -k "React开发" -k "Vue工程师" -p 2\n
        maimai search -k "研发" -m enterprise
    """
    from src.modules.export import export_candidates
    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    from src.services.pipeline import search_all_keywords, search_all_keywords_enterprise

    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)

    async def _search():
        reset_delay_session()
        browser = get_browser_manager()
        try:
            if mode == 'enterprise':
                all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
                    browser, keywords, company, max_pages
                )
            else:
                all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
                    browser, keywords, company, location, max_pages
                )

            # Apply auto-filter
            if auto_filter:
                from src.utils.filters import filter_candidates
                before_count = len(all_candidates)
                # Convert to dict for filtering
                candidates_dict = [
                    {
                        'maimai_id': c.maimai_id,
                        'name': c.name,
                        'title': c.title,
                        'company': c.company,
                        'location': c.location,
                    }
                    for c in all_candidates
                ]
                filtered_dict = filter_candidates(candidates_dict, auto_filter=True)

                # Filter original list
                filtered_ids = {c['maimai_id'] for c in filtered_dict}
                all_candidates = [c for c in all_candidates if c.maimai_id in filtered_ids]

                if len(all_candidates) < before_count:
                    console.print(f"[dim]自动过滤: {before_count} → {len(all_candidates)} 位候选人[/dim]\n")

            # Display combined results
            if all_candidates:
                table = Table(title=f"Search Results ({len(all_candidates)} candidates total)")
                table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
                table.add_column("Title", style="green")
                table.add_column("Company", style="yellow")
                table.add_column("Location", style="blue")
                table.add_column("ID", style="dim")

                for c in all_candidates[:30]:  # Show first 30
                    table.add_row(c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or '', c.maimai_id)

                console.print(table)

                if len(all_candidates) > 30:
                    console.print(f"[dim]... and {len(all_candidates) - 30} more[/dim]")
            else:
                console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")

            # Export if requested
            if export and all_candidates:
                fmt = 'csv' if export.endswith('.csv') else 'excel'
                export_candidates(format=fmt, output_path=export)

        finally:
            await browser.close()

    run_async(_search())
